1989_09_29-India-Mumbai-Faith-OT-st2

Radhanath Swami Transcriptions Template

Meta Data v8

Event Name/Title

Intricate Workings of The Modes of Material Nature

Event Date

29 Sep 1989

Event Location

Inida, Mumbai

Event Type

Charity

Transcriber

Sriram Patro

Completed

14 May 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-056-Faith%20(Mumbai)-OT-dt_89_09_29.mp3

Source Video

Verse

SB 11.25.06

Tags – Places

Tags – Pastimes

Lifting Govardhan

Tags – Persons

Krishna, Jesus Christ, Mohammed, Nicky, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu

Tags – Themes

Alcohol addiction, Alcoholics Anonymous , Faith, Sense Perception, Belief

Faith is beyond sense perception

I am very very fortunate with all of you today. I would like to thank all of you for coming I'd like to thank you for kindly inviting me. I was very moved by that story about the child in the blazing building but that is on fire. What saved the life of that child was faith. Faith in what was beyond his own sense perception. Today unfortunately in our educational institutions and in our society at large there are very much conditioned to only believe in that which we can perceive. Sometimes being a spiritual lecturer I travel many parts of the world I am often challenged, the common challenge can you show me God if you cannot show me God how can you expect me to believe it, after all I am a man of the 20th century. I believe what can be documented and what can be experienced directly.

99% of what we believe is beyond our sense perception

I was just two days back given that same challenge so I inquired from that person do you really believe that you only accept true what you can experience with your senses. I have a challenge to you, I challenge to be able to prove that 99% of you believe to be true you have never seen, you have never smelled, you have never touched, you have never tasted, you have never directly experienced. I asked him have you ever been to America he said no I said do you believe America exists, he said yes. I said what is your reference to establish the fact that America exists, he said there's books the geography there’s books on history and I met people of the net, but you have never directly seen, tasted or experienced America correct but you believe it's true, you believe and the basis of faith what you read in your books and what you hear from people who are saying who have been there. Have you seen Mohandas Gandhi, Mahatma Gandhi? He's the father of modern India, he say no, he left this world before I was born, so I asked him do you believe he lived he said of course. Why do you believe you've never seen him you believe it's a fact that he lived because there's history books and there's people who have met him. Factually, practically everything we believe to be true we have never experienced. The Sun looks like it's as big as my fan but do you accept its millions of times bigger than the earth of course you do because you read it in the books because you've heard it from scientists but you have no experience of that. I want to ask you a very personal question do you know who your father and mother are, he say yes of course I know I go home every night. How do you know that your mother and father I know Amm… were you watching at the time when they came together to conceive you. He said no you only believe the existence of your mother and father on the basis of faith behind your sense perception. Now when it comes to God we believe 99% of what we believe to be fact is beyond our sense perception, beyond what can be proven to us directly and when it comes to God we say if you cannot show me God right now or I can see I don't believe it. But the fact is there are books just like you have a geography and history books which describe the coming of God and how God is created this material existence and how he has sent his eternal associates to this world to uplift us to give us knowledge in him and today and throughout history there have been saintly persons in all the religions of the world who testified that they have seen God that they know God that they love God that there is a God.

Qualities of the person who have experience God

Now what are the qualities of those persons who state that there is a God? Let us look at the qualities of Lord Jesus Christ, let us look at the qualities of his Apostles like Paul and Peter, and St. Luke and the other great soul. St. Francis St. Benedict, St. Dominic if you read the history of any of these Saints lives you will find the exact same qualities. Very unique personalities but they all display the same qualities. Now if you look at the great Saints of India people like Ramanuja Acharya, Shankaracharya, Madhavachrya, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and if you read the script these are modern-day persons within the last five six hundred years.

Look at the Saints living today and you will find the exact same qualities, they are merciful, they are compassionate they are self-controlled, they are the friends of all the beings, they do not discriminate between man and woman rich or poor, black or white Indian Pakistani Russian or American. They love all living beings and they're willing to sacrifice their lives and die to help another at any time of the day or night. These are the constant qualities of every saintly person who has faith in God who has ever lived throughout this world. It is not a sectarian idea and if you look at the Saints of the Muslim tradition you will find the same qualities. In the Bible they asked Jesus Christ what is the first and Great Commandment he did not say to be a Christian he said to love God with all your heart all your mind and all your soul and to love your neighbour as yourself. And Lord Shri Krishna in Bhagavad-gita describes

sarva-dharmān parityajya
mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja
ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo
mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ

[Bg. 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

All Religion Speaks Love of Godhead

He says abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender to the Supreme God with love he shall protect you from all sinful reactions do not fear. Another word of religion means to love God. Religion means to surrender to God and religion means to extend that love to our neighbour and the fact is all living creatures are our neighbours without exception. If you love God you will naturally, spontaneously love every living creature because you will see the presence of God in everyone because everyone is the children of God. So these great souls throughout history all over the world who had no direct communication with one another it just so happens they all have essentially the same philosophy and they have the same benevolent loving compassionate qualities in their life. And you will find no one throughout the history of man without that faith in love and God who exhibited such a quantity of compassion and mercy and sacrifice to others.

Whom to be believe – Great Personalities or Academician

So are we to believe and have faith in these persons are we to believe and have faith in some academician who say that creation came from a big bang which we think is true but we cannot show you that we cannot prove that either to your sense perception. So faith is the most essential and most valuable asset to be obtained within our life because with faith there is nothing impossible. There is a saying that impossible is a word in a fool’s dictionary. Sometimes we are faced with problems in this world that seem how possibly can I overcome and often times and usually that problem is not something outside that we have to deal with it's something within our own hearts and even our own minds.

Alcoholism – A deadly Killer

Alcoholism is such a problem for example. When one becomes addicted to this very deadly and dangerous drug which is the biggest killer on earth it seems that is impossible to give it up. I say alcohol is the biggest killer on earth because it is. In the country that I am from the United States of America the largest single cause of death is automobile accidents, in 85% of all automobile accidents that are serious when I say serious I mean death isn't curved one of the members is intoxicated on alcohol. Therefore alcoholism is the largest greatest murderer in America then that is not comedy all the heart disease, all the violent crimes, 60% of all violent crimes in America are committed by people who are either under the influence of alcohol or drugs. A large percent of divorces are due to alcoholism and drug abuse within the family, now divorce means the mother and the father their lives are in shambles and the poor children. 80% of all juvenile delinquents in America are from families that were divorced and usually these children simply become horrible menaces to the society their home life and the miserable existence. So beyond just the horrible horrible pain and suffering we personal personally endure through alcoholism. How much it is destroying the lives of millions and millions of people every day in this world. And it is so difficult to overcome. In fact it is almost like it's a mountain within our heart and to give it up is like moving a mountain Haan !! But factually to some extent all the vices of this world are like mountains in our heart. Did you ever try to move a mountain? Huh you can push and push and push and you can get all your friends and all your relatives you can get the whole society to try to move that mountain. G

Govardhan – Beyond Sense Perception

There's one very famous mountain and place called Vrindavan. I feel it's fresh in my mind because just a few weeks ago I was there. This mountain is called Govardhan Hill, this mountain is 21 kilometres around it it's a very holy place and many Hindus and actually, people of all religions walk around this hill on pilgrimage. Now did you ever try to move a 21 kilometres mountain if everyone in India pushed that mountain that mountain would not move. How many people are now if all 800 million people gathered around that mountain to push it would not move. I guarantee but to speak of the mountains in the Himalayas. There is a story in the Srimad Bhagavatam, one of the great holy literatures of the Sanatana-Dharma religion of India. When Lord Krishna appeared on this earth there was a very devastating rainfall and it was about to completely drown everyone in the whole village of Vrindavan. Krishna is a great incarnation of God. When they called upon him helplessly that Krishna we have no shelter other than you, save us, he took that mountain and lifted it with the little finger of his left hand and held it for seven days to act as an umbrella. One may think this is a mythological story of how anyone can lift a mountain but for God who has created everything on earth and everything beyond this earth within this universe and all universes, is there any limitation to what God can do? In the Bible Lord Jesus Christ has spoken that if you have enough faith which is the size of a mustard seed the smallest of all seeds if you tell this mountain to move into the ocean the mountain must move to the ocean and it will move into the ocean this is the power of faith.

Faith in God Can move mountains

If we have faith in God this amount that seems to be a moveable for us within our heart of all of our attachments are of our vices all of our sinful inclinations it can be moved but on your own it is impossible. It is said a friend in need is a friend indeed haan… in this society friendship means I will scratch your back and you scratch my back as long as you're scratching I will scratch what if you stop scratching why should I waste my time with you Hmm.. The fact is nobody really knows who their friend is until they're in a disastrous crisis in our life, only then is it revealed who is a friend. When everything is going well or everything is alright it's easy to have so many. It's easy to be a friend and it's easy to have friend. But when everything falls from under you, when your life is at a disaster, when you lose everything, when there's no benefit for anyone to be with you who's going to stand by your side and help you, that person is a friend. And I must say that in my travels throughout the world rarely do I ever find the affiliation of real friends that I find in this organization of Alcoholics Anonymous. I know there are many persons who are of this stature who have these characteristics I know Nicky Foreman very personally I know how day or night anyone who is in need he will be right there by his side and he has nothing to gain materially except headaches, unregulated schedule, exhaustion, he gets no money he gets no adoration or distinction in society. There's only one gratification that he knows that he's pleasing God by trying to reach out and sacrifice his life to help another that is a friend.

And Alcoholics Anonymous as an organization is coming together regularly to try to develop this quality of friendship amongst each other and without friends like this we are in a hopeless condition because it is such friendship, it is through such friendship that faith is born within our heart. Faith comes by associating with someone who has that faith. It is said in the Bible by St. Paul that you cannot save yourself by worse alone it is by faith but faith without works is dead. So when we see practical living examples of people due to their faith in God are willing to sacrifice everything in anything to help another. That example of that person more than anything else in life gives birth to faith within our hearts. Alcoholism is a horrible horrible disease. It's practically an incurable disease and if actually the only people who are really successful in curing this disease is Alcoholics Anonymous and I'm speaking worldwide. Why, because this immovable mountain can only be moved by faith. We have to first admit that we are helpless, we have to admit that we are insignificant, we have to admit that we are completely dependent on a mercy of God. We have to not only admit it to ourselves but we have to admit it to others. That means we have to be thoroughly honest and straightforward to reveal what are our defects, what are our shortcomings, what are our sins and what are our faults. Any fool can extol his own glories Amm.., it does not take any integrity for a man to proudly boast what he has done and what he will do but it takes a very very great man or great woman to admit to oneself and before others one's false defects and weaknesses.

It doesn't take much of a person any fool can argue for the rest of his life that he's right but it takes great strength and courage to admit you're wrong. Now until we are really and ready to do this faith in God cannot truly awakened within our heart until we admit that we are helpless we have tried we have failed it is only by the mercy of my real friends who are the representatives of God in my life who are helping me to know and love God it is only by the mercy of God and them that I have any hope. We cannot have that consciousness until we humble ourselves and sometimes it is impossible to humble ourselves until we are put in a situation where we are kicked to the very very bottom of existence.

I was speaking to one man just the other day he was at the Alcoholics Anonymous meeting at the Murphy Law Center a few weeks ago. And he came to the temple where I am living to meet with me and he was explaining how he had a very very promising life before him. He was explaining that he had a nice family, he was a doctor and he had a good job. The world was at his feet but then somehow or other due to certain pressures in life and so on and so forth he became addicted to alcohol and he lost everything he lost his distinction, he practically lost his earnings, he had nothing in this world except pain, suffering and disgrace. In it somehow or rather a friend came to the bottom of this worldly existence to find him and pick him up by the hand and said please come take the hand of God and walk forward and that person was Nicky from Alcoholics Anonymous and now this man is a doctor he's sober. And he was explaining to me that becoming an alcoholic was the greatest blessing of God in my life because if didn’t become an alcoholic 24:43 to 24: 59 voice unclear I am very great yes I have my religion but as far as crying out for God there's no need they said that Because of this horrible condition of my life I have learned the greatest the greatest in all existence I have obtained the greatest fortune and I am convinced I have only depended on the mercy of God and my life is in his hands, the fulfillment of my life. So you see whatever it takes to bring us to this platform of faith and dependence on the mercy of God that is the greatest fortune. I have been to many churches, many synagogues, many mosques, and many temples to give lectures and to meet with people and to counsel people and I will say in other world there's hardly one church temple, synagogue or mosque it has a congregation of more sincere spiritual devotees of Bogdan Alcoholics Anonymous.

Religion is desperate cry out for mercy of God

Most people go to the temple to do their puja to perform their rituals because it's a tradition or because it's socially acceptable and because they know it should be done and they give their charity and they say the prayers are they chant their mantras or they listen to the sermon but it's done very superficially very ritualistically that is not religion. Religion is that sincere desperate cry out for the mercy of God that I am completely dependent on your mercy in my life that is what Jesus taught, if that is what Mohammad taught, that is what Krishna taught, that is what Moses taught.

In alcoholics Anonymous I find the most receptive people who want to here they don't want to hear simply because it's intellectually stimulating but from the bottom of their hearts they're reaching out for something to hold on to grab on to and bring them out of their distress in life you cannot be saved from the horrible condition of life through some ritual or some giving charity or some tradition it only comes when you desperately and hung with all humility fall at the feet of your supreme father beg for his mercy I can't do it without you. So in fact it is training this is not a sectarian religion but the fact of religion has nothing to do with sectarianism. But this is a religion because it is teaching people how to love God, how to depend on the mercy of God and how to call out for God and how to on behalf of God return that favour that has been given to you upon up. In giving we receive. A wealthy man is to be judged not by how much he has, that has nothing to do with wealth because that wealth never make one satisfied. The biggest multimillionaires in America are addicted to sleeping pills, psychiatrists, suicide and they're miserable people that is not wealth.

Wealth is determined by how much we can give

A wealthy man is to be judged by how much he is giving of himself or how much she is giving of herself to reach out to be a friend to others, that is wealth. Wealth is not determined by how much we can take is how much we can give. Jesus Christ had no money he had no house he had no property but who is more wealthy than him who gave more charity than him hann…and similarly in my own life my Guru he had no money he came to America at the age of 70 years old. He had eight dollars forty rupees and he couldn't even exchange it because rupees are useless outside of India. He had no place to live, he was living in the garage of New York and everyone was telling him in India, come home why are you staying out there why don't you come back? He said no no I have a mission these people are suffering and he reached out and touched millions of people throughout the world by granting them thank you God not sectarian faith that breezy hate and discontent toward others but that faith was breeds love and compassion by which you can see the essence of the universal principle of all realities. Although he had no money no property he was the wealthiest man I have met in life and my parents in America happened to be millionaires and I know hundreds and hundreds of families who are millionaires. I know some of the very wealthiest families in America their children the parents and this man was by far the wealthiest man I ever met because he had so much to give.

This Alcoholics Anonymous group is a great thing. Why because those who take it seriously come they will be given so much to give. What is taught as the essence is how to love how to sacrifice based on faith in God, because when we have faith in God our love becomes infinite. Our compassion becomes infinite. We are not infinite, we are finite tiny little creatures, we are like insects, when we admit we're just an insect, infinitesimal tiny and we open our hearts for the mercy of God then the infinite mercy of God can force in our heart to the world that is that is for sure.

Twelve Steps Followed in Alcoholics Anonymous Groups

So the twelve steps really haan…, most beautifully composed because they truly give the opportunity for anyone who takes these twelve seriously not only to overcome the problem of alcoholism but to overcome all the defects within our selfish character and to develop a pure heart you know I just said alcoholic it's not a bad man he is not a mad man but he's a sick man. That is one of your slogans. Well throughout this world everyone is, the terrorists they're sick men the murderers are sick men, the gross materialists were simply out of greed exploiting the resources of the world they are sick men. Anyone who has forgotten his real self, anyone who has forgotten his connection to God is a sick man no one in this world is a bad man no one in this world is a mad man this is a sick world read the newspapers you'll see sickness everywhere. For you people the fact that you're sitting in this room today makes you glorious because you're willing to admit your sickness and you're willing to humble yourself to take the treatment. The twelve steps begin to admit that we are sick, we admit we were powerless over alcohol that our lives had become unmanageable. We came to believe that a power greater than ourselves could restore us to sanity man. Making the decision to turn our will and our lives over to the care of God, as we understood. Made a searching and fearless moral inventory than ourselves admitted to God to ourselves and to another human being the exact nature of our mind we're highly ready to have God we need all these defects of character. Humbly asked him to remove our shortcomings maybe less than all persons we had harmed and became willing to make amends to them all. Made direct amends to such people wherever possible except when to do so would injure them or others, continue to take personal inventory and when we were wrong promptly admitted. Soft prayer and meditation to improve our conscious contact with God as we understood him. Praying only for knowledge of his will for us in the power to carry that out. Having had a spiritual awakening as a result of these steps we tried to carry this message to alcoholics and to practice these principles in all affairs. Recently I was talking to a very very saintly person, a very religious person and I was explained in a little about Alcoholics Anonymous and I read the twelve steps to him and he said my god he said I wish I could follow those twelve steps. Imagine what a great time with how much greater of a saint I could be if I could follow these twelve steps. Really the essence of all of these procedures is humility. Jesus Christ said the meek shall inherit the earth. There is no greater strength and humility, there is no greater courage than the ability to be humble and all circumstances, because in that humility all the power and all the glory of God is behind us, to pick us up to uplift us, to protect us in all circumstances of life. So my humble request to all of you before I close this short this afternoon please do not fall into the same unfortunate condition of most of the religions of the world today including the one that I follow and that is to follow ritualistically superficially simply because we're supposed to. But with the sincere heart with a desperate heart we should actually take shelter of each and every one of these twelve steps we should ponder deep in our heart each one of these twelve steps what it means in how our life depends on it and it is guaranteed that anyone who follows these twelve steps will find a kingdom of God within his heart.

All Religion speaks the importance of Holy Name

That I could promise you why because all the great Saints have promised us. In this age and all the great religions of the world it is declared that there is no better process of calling upon God and invoking his divine presence than chanting his names in the Bible look King David said chant the names of God and he will be saved and in the new testament it is said hallow be the name of God in the Quran there are innumerable references of these how it is absolutely essential to cry out the name of Allah and in the Vedas it is considered the Yuga Dharma

harer nāma harer nāma
harer nāmaiva kevalam
kalau nāsty eva nāsty eva
nāsty eva gatir anyathā

[Cc. Madhya 6.242]

[In this age of quarrel and hypocrisy, the only means of deliverance is the chanting of the holy names of the Lord. There is no other way. There is no other way. There is no other way.]

In this age of quarrel and hypocrisy that we are all living there is no better way to invoke the divine presence of God than chanting his name. When we chant the name of God we come in the presence of God when we chant the name of God all the mercy of God is upon us and we chant the name of God all these great mountains within our hearts that are removable convenient this microphone could be moved so simple the mountains of your heart can be moved like that but we have to chant the name of God in humility. We have to cry out the name of God in desperation in complete dependence just as in Mahabharata you have seen how Draupadi when she was in a completely hopeless condition of life being stripped naked in front of all these distinguished men and she realized there was no power of her own to save herself from this humiliation she raised her arms in the air and she cried out in hopelessness, helplessness and faith she cried out Hey Krishna !!! Hey Govinda !!! Do not think that we are in any different condition than Draupadi if we cry out with the same sincere feeling she did God will be there to save us from all the problems of this world and lift us up to his eternal Kingdom. So you are very fortunate that you have each other and that you have the mercy of God waiting it to your doorstep.

Thank you very much.
